items.text: Class Q grants planning permission for the change of use of certain agricultural buildings — and some former agricultural buildings — together with land within their curtilage, to use as Class C3 dwellinghouses. It also covers the building operations reasonably necessary to convert the building... and, since the 21st of May, 2024, limited single-storey rear extensions. <break time="0.6s" /> The right exists to release redundant farm buildings for housing — without testing the merits of residential use in the countryside against the development plan. <break time="0.6s" /> And it is the most litigated, and the most technically demanding, of the Part 3 rights. It generates a disproportionate share of prior approval appeals. It rewards forensic preparation more than any other permitted development right — because eligibility turns on history, fact, and structure... which must be evidenced, rather than asserted.
